---
title: "SIMD algorithms for excess queries"
subtitle: "A Subtitle If Needed"

author:
  - name: Nikolay Malkovsky
    email: malkovskynv@gmail.com
    orcid: "0000-0001-8571-6857"
    affiliation:
      name: University Name
      city: City
      country: Country

acm-metadata:
  final: true
  acmart-options: "sigconf"
  copyright-year: 2026
  acm-year: 2026
  copyright: acmcopyright
  doi: XXXXXXX.XXXXXXX
  conference-acronym: "Conference '26"
  conference-name: Conference Name
  conference-date: June 03--05, 2026
  conference-location: City, Country
  price: "0.00"
  isbn: 978-X-XXXX-XXXX-X/XX/XX
  keywords:
    - succinct data structures
    - wavelet trees
    - rank/select
  abstract: |
    This is a template paper for the Pixie succinct data structures library.
    Replace this abstract with your actual abstract.

date: today
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  acm-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
    biblio-style: ACM-Reference-Format
---

# Introduction

Introduce the problem and motivate the need for compact data structures.
Cite foundational work such as @jacobson1989 and @navarro2016.

# Preliminaries

Define notation and background concepts.

## Rank and Select

Given a bitvector $B[1..n]$ with $m$ ones:

- $\text{rank}_1(B, i)$ returns the number of ones in $B[1..i]$
- $\text{select}_1(B, j)$ returns the position of the $j$-th one

Both operations can be supported in $O(1)$ time with $o(n)$ bits of
extra space [@clark1996].

## Wavelet Trees

The wavelet tree [@grossi2003] is a compact data structure that supports
rank, select, and access on sequences over arbitrary alphabets in
$O(\log \sigma)$ time, where $\sigma$ is the alphabet size.

# Method

Describe your approach here.

## Construction Algorithm

```cpp
// Example: include a code snippet
void build(const std::span<const uint64_t>& data) {
    // Implementation details
}
```

## Space Analysis

The total space usage is $n + o(n)$ bits, where $n$ is the input size.

# Experiments

## Setup

Describe experimental methodology.

## Results

# Conclusion

Summarize contributions and future work.

# Acknowledgments

Acknowledgments go here.

# References
